_readme: Copy this file to ~/.agent/config.json and fill in your API keys.

providers._readme: api_format must be 'anthropic' or 'openai'. api_key can be a literal value or '$ENV_VAR' to read from environment.

qwen._readme: Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint works — Qwen, Groq, Together, local vLLM, etc.

ollama._readme: Local Ollama — set api_key to any non-empty string (auth not required).

memory._readme: tidy_interval_seconds: how often (in seconds) the agent auto-reorganises memory. tidy_file_threshold: minimum number of new memory files before a tidy is triggered.

context._readme: Intelligent context manager. Split into 'storage' (what to keep) and 'consolidation' (when/how to compress).

_readme: max_categories: upper limit on dynamic LTM categories.
When exceeded, the agent merges two similar ones via LLM (default 15).
decay_factor: per-sleep importance multiplier (0.0–1.0, default 0.95).
Lower = faster forgetting. Entries below 0.05 are pruned automatically.
token_ratio: fraction of the provider's max_tokens at which a sleep cycle fires (default 0.70).
max_tokens itself is set in providers.<active_provider>.max_tokens — not here.
keep_last_messages: number of recent messages retained after sleep compression (default 6).
idle_seconds: seconds of inactivity before the background idle-consolidation task fires (default 300).
The idle task polls every 30 s and only triggers when the dirty flag is set (i.e. new messages exist).
min_messages: minimum messages in working memory before any consolidation is attempted (default 4).
token_estimation: ratios used to estimate token counts from character counts.
chars_per_token: non-CJK characters per token (Latin/ASCII, default 4).
cjk_chars_per_token: CJK characters per token (Hanzi/Kana/Hangul, default 1).
Increase chars_per_token for shorter English text or code-heavy sessions.
Increase cjk_chars_per_token if consolidation triggers too aggressively for Chinese/Japanese.

mcp_servers._readme: Remove this object and add real MCP server entries. Each entry needs name, command, and args.
mcp_servers.name: filesystem

evolution._readme: Self-improvement plugin. Set enabled=false to disable session scoring and behavioral rule learning entirely.

_readme: Persistent scheduled task service.
poll_seconds: how often the scheduler checks for due tasks.
lease_seconds: how long a claimed run is considered owned before stale-run recovery may requeue it.

plugins._readme: Per-plugin enable/disable. Use the plugin name as key. Set enabled=false to disable a plugin without removing it.

_system_prompt_file_readme: null = use the built-in prompt. Set to an absolute path to load a custom system prompt from a .md or .txt file, e.g. '/Users/you/.agent/my_prompt.md'.
_tavily_api_key_readme: Optional. Used by the built-in tavily_search tool. Can be a literal key or '$ENV_VAR'. Leave empty or null to disable Tavily-backed search.
_output_dir_readme: null = use default ~/.agent/output. Set to an absolute path to override. Supports ~ and $ENV expansion. All tool/MCP/skill outputs (screenshots, exports, temp files) go here. Subdirectories are created on demand.

channels._readme: External channel configuration. Only one channel may be active at a time. If no channel is enabled the CLI is used by default.

_readme: Feishu/Lark bot via WebSocket long connection (no public IP required).
If running from the repo: uv sync --extra feishu && uv run simple gateway
If using a globally installed uv tool: uv tool install --reinstall --editable . --with lark-oapi
1. Create a Feishu app at https://open.feishu.cn/app
2. Enable 'Bot' capability and subscribe to 'im.message.receive_v1' event
3. Copy App ID and App Secret below
app_id / app_secret  : from Feishu Open Platform > Credentials & Basic Info
encrypt_key          : optional; set if 'Encrypt Key' is enabled in event subscription
verification_token   : optional; set if 'Verification Token' is enabled
allow_from           : list of open_ids allowed to use the bot; empty = allow all
react_emoji          : emoji reaction added when a message is received (THUMBSUP, OK, EYES, DONE …)
group_policy         : 'mention' = only respond when @mentioned; 'open' = respond to all group messages
